blurb: Apple's Bluetooth implementation uses Secure Simple Pairing with ECDH P-256 for Classic Bluetooth, generating a link key used to derive AES-CCM session encryption and integrity-protection keys. Bluetooth Low Energy uses LE Secure Connections with P-256 ECDH and AES-CCM-128 for connection encryption. Cross-transport key derivation (CTKD) allows a single pairing ceremony to establish keys for both BR/EDR and BLE transports. BLE devices use randomized advertising addresses rotating periodically to prevent passive tracking. Applications access Bluetooth only through Core Bluetooth, which restricts raw device identifier access and requires user authorization for background operation.
